“Scanner” — the ergonomic hand-scanner form (ADR-0003)

Magic-Mouse-inspired, more ergonomic · ambidextrous teardrop · no buttons · concept renders from the parametric model
86 × 54 × 24 mmlow prow → rear palm arch Ambidextrousbilateral symmetry <90 gtarget Pocket · stick · hang · deskall four carries kept
Hero — prow window (glass band), crown touch dish, glow skirt
Profile — the grip story: fingertips on the prow flanks, palm on the arch
Rear — palm arch; USB-C in the skirt; GNSS window at the crest
Top — teardrop plan; dish; prow band; glow ring
Nose-on — liquid sample port at the tip (dip to sample)

How you use it

  • Grasp: arch fills the palm, thumb + fingers land on the flanks — identical either hand. Orientation is instant: the nose points.
  • Sweep to scan: prow optics aim forward-down; IMU pose-stamps every frame → super-resolution maps from a hand sweep.
  • Touch to read: fingertip in the crown dish (PPG/ECG); squeeze the flanks to confirm; rim swipe to dismiss.
  • Dip to sample: nose tip touches a droplet — capillary port wicks it in (v2 fluidics; port is buffered in v1).
  • Set down / stick / hang: flat base with dock ring (magnetic mounts + pogo accessories), desk-native, lanyard bore in the rear skirt.

Engineering notes (honest)

  • Board reshapes at H3: rectangular 60×46 fits the mid/rear shell; the low prow takes a thin angled optical sub-board/rigid-flex (sensing face rotates forward). Zone-packer gains a teardrop outline mode.
  • Fan exhaust moves to the rear skirt; GNSS to the crest; everything else carries over (ADR-0002 set unchanged).
  • Renders are concept-grade; H5 does production surfacing. STEP + STL in enclosure/.
